ticket.subject: Fwd: EU De Minimis Update – New Requirements Effective July 1, 2026
ticket.raw_subject: Fwd: EU De Minimis Update – New Requirements Effective July 1, 2026
ticket.description:
> Begin forwarded message:
> 
> From: <bvardehzadeh@ups.com>
> Subject: EU De Minimis Update – New Requirements Effective July 1, 2026
> Date: 17 June 2026 at 4:24:11 AM IST
> To: <vimal@pluginhive.com>, <sanoop@pluginhive.com>, <abhilash@pluginhive.com>
> 
> Dear Pluginhive, 
>  
> Effective July 1, 2026, the European Union will remove the current €150 de minimis threshold. As a result, all shipments—regardless of value—will require full customs clearance. To support compliance, UPS is introducing new data requirements and enhancements across shipping systems. Please review your current shipping setup to ensure the following data elements can be captured and transmitted.
>  
> B2B/B2C:
> July 1 requirement by EU regulatory authorities. While the Ship API will not enforce this field as mandatory (to avoid shipment failures), the data is still required for customs clearance.  If this information is not provided, shipments will be held at customs and risk return to shipper (RTS), with any associated costs billed back to the partner.
>  
> This is an existing field listed as “ConsigneeTypeValue” in the Developer Portal: ShipmentRequest/Shipment/GlobalTaxInformation/ConsigneeTypeValue
> This update should be implemented as soon as possible, as the July 1 requirement applies based oncustoms arrival date, not label manifest date.
>  
> Product ID: 
> The Product ID fields have been postponed until November 1, 2026. Additional details will be shared once finalized in the Ship API.
>  
> IOSS: 
> IOSS data requirements have been updated. As of July1, the IOSS name and address fields are no longer required on the commercial invoice. Only the IOSS number is required to properly ship using IOSS.
>  
> What This Means for You
> To ensure compliance and avoid disruption:
> Review and update shipping workflows to support Business Indicators
> Enable capture of Product IDs (in preparation for future enforcement)
> Update systems to support expanded IOSS data fields
>  
> Important:
> Incomplete or missing data may result in:
> Customs clearance delays
> Shipment holds or returns
>  
> We will follow up to assess your readiness and ensure a smooth transition and positive cross-border experience.
>  
> If you have any questions or would like to discuss next steps, please don’t hesitate to contact me.
>  
> Sincerely, 
>  
> Benita Vardehzadeh
> UPS Digital Access Program (DAP)
> International Marketing Senior Manager
> Global Strategy and Expansion
> bvardehzadeh@ups.com <mailto:bvardehzadeh@ups.com>
> Remote in US PST
> +1-657-3859640

comments.html_body:
Begin forwarded message:

From: <bvardehzadeh@ups.com>
Subject: EU De Minimis Update – New Requirements Effective July 1, 2026
Date: 17 June 2026 at 4:24:11 AM IST
To: <vimal@pluginhive.com>, <sanoop@pluginhive.com>, <abhilash@pluginhive.com>

Dear Pluginhive, 
 
Effective July 1, 2026, the European Union will remove the current €150 de minimis threshold. As a result, all shipments—regardless of value—will require full customs clearance. To support compliance, UPS is introducing new data requirements and enhancements across shipping systems. Please review your current shipping setup to ensure the following data elements can be captured and transmitted.
 
B2B/B2C:
July 1 requirement by EU regulatory authorities. While the Ship API will not enforce this field as mandatory (to avoid shipment failures), the data is still required for customs clearance.  If this information is not provided, shipments will be held at customs and risk return to shipper (RTS), with any associated costs billed back to the partner.
 
  • This is an existing field listed as “ConsigneeTypeValue” in the Developer Portal: ShipmentRequest/Shipment/GlobalTaxInformation/ConsigneeTypeValue
  • This update should be implemented as soon as possible, as the July 1 requirement applies based oncustoms arrival date, not label manifest date.
 
Product ID: 
The Product ID fields have been postponed until November 1, 2026. Additional details will be shared once finalized in the Ship API.
 
IOSS: 
IOSS data requirements have been updated. As of July1, the IOSS name and address fields are no longer required on the commercial invoice. Only the IOSS number is required to properly ship using IOSS.
 
What This Means for You
To ensure compliance and avoid disruption:
  • Review and update shipping workflows to support Business Indicators
  • Enable capture of Product IDs (in preparation for future enforcement)
  • Update systems to support expanded IOSS data fields
 
Important:
Incomplete or missing data may result in:
  • Customs clearance delays
  • Shipment holds or returns
 
We will follow up to assess your readiness and ensure a smooth transition and positive cross-border experience.
 
If you have any questions or would like to discuss next steps, please don’t hesitate to contact me.
 
Sincerely, 
 
Benita Vardehzadeh
UPS Digital Access Program (DAP)
International Marketing Senior Manager
Global Strategy and Expansion
bvardehzadeh@ups.com
Remote in US PST
+1-657-3859640

comments.body:
Raised L3:

https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1781698402961519

UPS has shared an update regarding upcoming EU customs regulation changes effective July 1, 2026.

Key points from the communication:
• A B2B/B2C indicator (ConsigneeTypeValue) will be required for EU shipments. UPS mentioned that although the API will not make this field mandatory, missing data may result in customs delays, shipment holds, or returns.
• Product ID requirements have been postponed until November 1, 2026.
• For IOSS shipments, only the IOSS Number will be required going forward. IOSS Name and Address will no longer be required.

Could you please review and confirm:
• Whether any changes are required on our end to comply with these requirements.
• If any action is needed before July 1, 2026.

Please find the UPS communication attached for reference.
comments.html_body:

Key points from the communication:
• A B2B/B2C indicator (ConsigneeTypeValue) will be required for EU shipments. UPS mentioned that although the API will not make this field mandatory, missing data may result in customs delays, shipment holds, or returns.
• Product ID requirements have been postponed until November 1, 2026.
• For IOSS shipments, only the IOSS Number will be required going forward. IOSS Name and Address will no longer be required.
 
Could you please review and confirm:
• Whether any changes are required on our end to comply with these requirements.
• If any action is needed before July 1, 2026.
 
Please find the UPS communication attached for reference.
